name: Furadouro and Ria de Aveiro from Espinho — circular route
name_original: Furadouro e Ria de Aveiro a partir de Espinho — percurso circular
description: Cycle 47.4 miles (76.2 km) on this moderate touring route from Espinho, exploring the Atlantic coast and Ria de Aveiro lagoons.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Espinho are primarily characterized by their coastal setting along Portugal's Atlantic coast. The landscape features extensive sandy beaches, coastal dunes, and generally flat terrain, making it suitable for various cycling abilities. A network of dedicated cycle paths, including sections of the EuroVelo 1 and the Coastal Ecovia, provides accessible routes along the seafront and through natural reserves. The region offers a mix of paved paths and wooden boardwalks, connecting urban areas with natural beauty spots. Best touring cycling routes around Espinho The most popular touring cycling route is Atlantic Coast Route: Espinho to Vila Nova de Gaia, an easy 20.6 miles (33.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 57 minutes to complete. This route follows the coastline, offering views of the Atlantic Ocean and connecting to nearby towns. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is EuroVelo 1 - Atlantic Coast Route, a moderate 42.7 miles (68.7 km) path. This segment of the EuroVelo 1 provides a longer coastal journey through diverse scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Chapel of Senhor da Pedra (Chapel of the Lord of the Rock) – Aguda Dunes Park loop from Espinho, a 13.4 miles (21.5 km) trail leading through coastal dunes and past a notable seaside chapel, often completed in about 1 hour 17 minutes. Touring cycling around Espinho is defined by coastal paths, extensive beaches, and seafront promenades. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to longer, moderate journeys. The routes in Espinho are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 120 reviews. More than 1000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Espinho's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Espinho?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes around Espinho, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The region's extensive network includes coastal paths, sections of the EuroVelo 1, and routes through natural reserves.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Espinho?
A: Yes, Espinho is well-suited for beginners and families, with 25 easy routes available. Many of these follow the flat coastal terrain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1385123500/atlantic-coast-route-espinho-to-vila-nova-de-gaia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atlantic Coast Route: Espinho to Vila Nova de Gaia&lt;/a&gt;, which offers gentle gradients and scenic ocean views. The Coastal Ecovia also provides accessible pathways.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while cycling around Espinho?
A: Touring cycling routes in Espinho showcase diverse natural landscapes. You'll encounter extensive sandy beaches, coastal dunes, and the unique environment of the Barrinha de Esmoriz Nature Reserve. The region is also part of the EuroVelo 1 - Atlantic Coast Route, offering varied coastal and natural scenery.

Q: Are there any challenging or advanced touring cycling routes in Espinho?
A: While Espinho is known for its generally flat coastal terrain, there are 13 routes classified as difficult for those seeking more of a challenge. These routes might involve longer distances or slightly more varied elevation, providing a good workout while still enjoying the region's beauty.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Espinho?
A: Yes, you can find several circular routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1386857814/furadouro-and-ria-de-aveiro-from-espinho-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Furadouro and Ria de Aveiro from Espinho — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to explore the coastline and the Ria de Aveiro lagoon system before returning to Espinho. Another popular loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32227577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel of Senhor da Pedra (Chapel of the Lord of the Rock) – Aguda Dunes Park loop from Espinho&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable attractions or landmarks I can visit along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by significant landmarks. The iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3238970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel of Senhor da Pedra (Chapel of the Lord of the Rock)&lt;/a&gt;, famously surrounded by the sea at high tide, is a must-see. You can also cycle through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6309350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguda Dunes Park&lt;/a&gt; and near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/573237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrinha de Esmoriz Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, offering opportunities to enjoy nature and local wildlife.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Espinho?
A: The touring cycling routes in Espinho are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 120 reviews. Cyclists often praise the well-maintained coastal paths, the stunning ocean views, and the accessibility of routes for various skill levels.

Q: Is it possible to reach the cycling routes using public transport?
A: Espinho is well-connected by public transport, particularly by train, making it easy to access the starting points of many cycling routes. The train station is centrally located, allowing for convenient travel with your bike to and from the area.

Q: Are there good parking options near the touring cycling trails in Espinho?
A: Yes, Espinho offers various parking options, especially along the seafront and near the town center. Many of the coastal routes are easily accessible from these parking areas, providing convenient starting points for your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Espinho?
A: The mild climate of Espinho makes touring cycling enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds. Summer is also popular, though it can be warmer, and the coastal breeze provides comfort. Winters are mild but can be wetter.

Q: Are there any routes that are particularly scenic or offer great viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The entire Coastal Ecovia and sections of the EuroVelo 1 offer continuous ocean views and picturesque stretches along the beaches and dunes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32318056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bocamar Beach ⛱️ – Granja Beach 🏖️ Cycle Path loop from Espinho&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent choice for scenic coastal vistas.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, Espinho and the charming coastal towns and villages along the routes offer numerous cafes, restaurants, and beach bars. These provide ample opportunities to stop for refreshments, enjoy local cuisine, and take a break with ocean views.
tips: Wide sandy beach fronted by some pretty uninspired vacation architecture. The bike trail benefits from a unfortunately ineffective and short post-modern shade structure.

On this bridge you can cross the Esmoriz lagoon. The wooden bridge fits perfectly into the landscape. Alternatively, you can walk around the lagoon on a beautiful path. It's best to drive in a circle so that you've seen everything. :)

Praia da Barrinha in Esmoriz is a fascinating place, very popular with surfers. Although it requires care from bathers due to the dangerous sea, it is possible to enjoy it by taking the necessary precautions. It is famous for the palaces that cross the dunes.

The dense forests along the coast are a great environmental asset. Originally a natural defense against the storms and sand dunes drifting inlands, they are now mostly replaced by pine plantations. Still, nice to bike through green nature and have some shade (at least when the sun stands lower). The smoothly paved bike trail runs parallel to the road but safely separate.
At the North end of the main beach esplanade the trails continue for the more remote beaches, most are accessible by bike via wooden boardwalks. Excellent public warm showers and restrooms.
Alternative road parallel to EN 109 that offers a few kilometers quieter. Furthermore, it runs through corn fields, making the landscape even more picturesque.

Estarreja is known for the Ria de Aveiro, the annual Carnival and Foral do Antuã of D. Manuel I and for its unique urban art.

At the ESTAU Festival, started by the artist Bordallo II, artists from all over the world transform the city with murals and installations inspired by local culture. In the link below you can find all the murals in Estarreja:
https://www.cm-estarreja.pt/estau

Ovar, blessed by nature, presents varied scenarios with the estuary, sea and forest. Highlights include Blue Flag beaches, the biodiversity of the Buçaquinho Environmental Park and outdoor activities such as surfing and stand-up paddleboarding. The cultural richness is evident in the decorative tiles and religious heritage, with emphasis on the Churches of Válega and Cortegaça. The municipality is also known for its vibrant cultural and gastronomic life, including the famous Pão-de-Ló de Ovar.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 76 km (47 miles) long and generally takes around 4 to 5 hours to complete by touring bicycle, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly paved and relatively flat, making it suitable for touring bicycles. While mostly smooth, some segments, particularly along the Ria de Aveiro, might include unpaved sections or boardwalks, adding variety to the ride.

faqs.question: Is the Furadouro and Ria de Aveiro circular route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While classified as 'moderate' due to its length, the route's mostly flat and paved surfaces make it accessible for cyclists with good basic fitness. It's generally suitable for all skill levels who are comfortable with longer distances.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a diverse landscape, from the refreshing Atlantic coast and extensive pine forests to the unique ecosystem of the Ria de Aveiro lagoons, known for their vast blue waters and abundant birdlife. The route also passes through charming towns like Ovar, offering glimpses into local life and architecture.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the beautiful Fishermen's Beach and Praia do Furadouro, the scenic Dune Forest of Ovar, and the Road Between Cornfields in Ria de Aveiro. You can also explore the Urban Art Murals of Estarreja and the historic Ovar Town Centre.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route is enjoyable for much of the year, but the spring and autumn months (April-May and September-October) offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds. Summer can be warm, especially inland, but the coastal sections provide a refreshing breeze. Winter can be cooler and wetter, but still cyclable on many days.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, a significant portion of this route (42%) follows sections of the EuroVelo 1 - Atlantic Coast Route - part Portugal 16. It also incorporates parts of the Linha da Praia (Furadouro <-> Esmoriz) and the Linha Nascente, among others.

faqs.question: Where can I park when cycling the Furadouro and Ria de Aveiro route?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in Espinho, you can look for public parking options within the town. Specific parking areas near the coastline or train station in Espinho are good starting points, though availability may vary, especially during peak season.

faqs.question: Is the Furadouro and Ria de Aveiro circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While cycling routes themselves don't typically have strict dog restrictions, it's important to consider the length and nature of this route. If bringing a dog, ensure it's accustomed to long rides (e.g., in a bike trailer) and always keep it on a leash when off the bike, especially in populated areas or near wildlife habitats like the Ria de Aveiro. Always check local regulations for beaches and specific natural reserves.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Furadouro and Ria de Aveiro circular route. It uses public roads and dedicated cycling paths that are freely accessible.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see along the Ria de Aveiro section?
faqs.answer: The Ria de Aveiro is a rich wetland ecosystem, particularly known for its diverse birdlife. You might spot various species of waterfowl, waders, and even flamingos, especially during migration seasons. Keep an eye out for them in the lagoons and marshy areas.

Road Between Cornfields in Ria de Aveiro
